recommendations: hook_bypass_via_absolute_grep_path remains structurally unresolved — hook does not intercept /usr/bin/grep invocations; this is a tooling gap not a behavioral one; no new behavioral recommendation until the hook is patched to cover absolute-path grep invocations

re_edit_thrash and optimistic_edit_without_read are both at 5-unit counts with zero improvement across 26+ cycles and trace to the same root cause (editing without reading current file state first); a single mandatory pre-edit Read step would address both simultaneously — no new recommendation beyond this existing one since it has not been implemented

declared_ready_without_runtime_exercise: self-documentation ritual executes correctly every time but violation precedes it every time; ritual is post-hoc not preventive; no new recommendation — existing pattern entry in mistake-patterns.md is thorough and correct but insufficient; a pre-declaration gate requiring explicit browser verification before typing ready/ship/push would intercept the violation before propagation
